en: When he went to school he was always the whipping-boy and always the object of his schoolfellows' mockery. The patient considers that his peculiar dialect was to blame for this. Later he was apprenticed to a severe and unkind master, under most trying conditions, from which all the other apprentices had run away, finding them intolerable. Here he held out for over two years. At fifteen the assault already mentioned took place, in addition to some other slighter homosexual experiences. Then fate sent him to France. There he made the acquaintance of a man from the South of France, a great boaster and Don Juan. He dragged the patient into a brothel; he went unwilling and out of fear. He was impotent there. Later he went to Paris, where his brother, a master-mason, the replica of his father, was leading a dissolute life. There the patient remained a long time, badly paid and helping his sister-in-law out of pity. The brother often took him along to a brothel, where the patient was always impotent. Here the brother asked him to make over to him his inheritance, 6000 francs. He first consulted his second brother, who was also in Paris, who urgently tried to dissuade him from giving the money to his brother, because it would only be squandered. Nevertheless the patient gave his all to his brother, who indeed soon squandered it. And the second brother, who would have dissuaded him, was also let in for 500 francs. To my astonished question why he had so light-heartedly given the money to his brother without any guarantee, he replied: he had asked for it, he was not a bit sorry about the money; he would give him another 6000 francs if he had it. The eldest brother came to grief altogether and his wife divorced him. The patient returned to Switzerland and remained for a year without regular employment, often suffering from hunger. During this time he made the acquaintance of a family where he became a frequent visitor. The husband belonged to some peculiar sect; he was a hypocrite and neglected his family. The wife was elderly, ill and weak, and moreover pregnant. There were six children and great poverty. The patient developed warm affection for this woman and divided with her the little he possessed. She brought him her troubles, and said she felt sure she would die in childbed. Then he promised her (he who possessed nothing) to take charge of the children himself and bring them up. The wife did die in childbed. The orphanage-board interfered, however, and allowed him only one child. So he had a child but no family, and naturally could not bring it up by himself. He thus came to think of marrying. But as he had never been in love with any woman he was in great perplexity. It then occurred to him that his elder brother was divorced from his wife, and he resolved to marry her. He wrote his intention to her in Paris. She was seventeen years older than he, but not disinclined to the plan. She invited him to come to Paris to talk matters over. On the eve of this journey fate, however, willed that he should run a big iron nail into his foot so that he could not travel. After a little while, when the wound was healed, he went to Paris, and found that he had imagined his sister-in-law, and now his fiancée , to be younger and prettier than she really was. The wedding took place, and three months later the first coitus, at his wife's initiative. He himself had no desire for it. They brought up the child together, he in the Swiss and she in the French way, for she was a French woman. At the age of nine the child was run over and killed by a cyclist. The patient then felt very lonely and dismal at home. He proposed to his wife that she should adopt a young girl, whereupon she broke out into a fury of jealousy. Then for the first time he fell in love with a young girl, whilst at the same time the neurosis started, with deep depression and nervous exhaustion, for meanwhile his life at home had become a hell.
zh: 當他上學時，總是成為代罪羔羊，也總是同學嘲弄的對象。病人認為，這要歸咎於他特有的方言。後來，他給一位嚴厲且不友善的師傅當學徒，處境極為艱難，其他學徒都因無法忍受而逃走了。他在那裡堅持了兩年多。十五歲時，除了其他一些較輕微的同性戀經歷外，還發生了前面提到的那次攻擊。然後，命運將他送到法國。在那裡，他結識了一個來自法國南部的男人，這個人極度自誇，是個唐璜。他拖著病人進了妓院；病人出於恐懼，不情願地去了。他在那裡陽痿。後來他去了巴黎，他的哥哥——一個泥水匠師傅，也是他父親的翻版——在那裡過著放蕩的生活。病人留在那裡很長一段時間，報酬微薄，並出於同情幫助他的嫂子。哥哥經常帶他去妓院，病人總是在那裡陽痿。這時，哥哥要求他把自己的遺產——六千法郎——轉讓給他。他先諮詢了也在巴黎的二哥，二哥極力勸阻他不要把錢給哥哥，因為那隻會被揮霍掉。儘管如此，病人還是把他所有的錢都給了哥哥，而哥哥果然很快就揮霍一空。那位曾勸阻他的二哥，也被騙走了五百法郎。當我驚訝地問他，為何如此輕率地把錢給哥哥而沒有任何擔保時，他回答說：是他要的，他對這筆錢一點也不後悔；如果他還有錢，他會再給他六千法郎。大哥最終徹底破產，他的妻子也與他離婚了。病人回到瑞士，有一年沒有固定工作，經常挨餓。在此期間，他結識了一個家庭，並成為那裡的常客。丈夫屬於某個奇怪的教派；他是個偽君子，忽略了自己的家庭。妻子年長、多病、虛弱，而且還懷了孕。家裡有六個孩子，非常貧窮。病人對這個女人產生了深厚的感情，並與她分享自己僅有的一點東西。她向他傾訴煩惱，並說她確信自己會死於難產。於是，他（一個一無所有的人）向她承諾，會親自照顧並撫養這些孩子。妻子果然死於難產。然而，孤兒委員會介入，只允許他帶走一個孩子。於是他有了一個孩子，卻沒有家庭，自然無法獨自撫養。因此，他開始考慮結婚。但由於他從未愛過任何女人，他感到非常困惑。這時，他想起大哥已經與妻子離婚，便決定娶她。他將這個打算寫信告訴了在巴黎的她。她比他年長十七歲，但對這個計劃並不反對。她邀請他去巴黎面談。然而，就在出發前夕，命運卻讓他腳上扎進了一根大鐵釘，使他無法成行。過了一陣子，傷口癒合後，他去了巴黎，發現他心目中的嫂子——現在是他的未婚妻——比他想像中要更老、更不漂亮。婚禮舉行了，三個月後，在他妻子的主動下，有了第一次性交。他自己對此並無慾望。他們一起撫養那個孩子，他用瑞士方式，她用法國方式，因為她是法國女人。孩子九歲時，被一個騎自行車的人撞死。病人隨後在家裡感到非常孤獨和沮喪。他建議妻子收養一個年輕女孩，她卻勃然大怒，嫉妒萬分。這時，他第一次愛上了一個年輕女孩，與此同時，他的神經症也開始了，伴隨著深深的憂鬱和神經衰弱，因為此時他的家庭生活已變成了地獄。
